summary: PR 1113 re-linked the Dolphin DSP library by marking `dolphin/dsp/dsp.c` and `dolphin/dsp/dsp_task.c` as `Matching` in `configure.py`. The only source changes in `dsp_task.c` converted two `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` guards to `#ifndef BUGFIX`, preserving match-only code in normal matching builds while allowing bugfix builds to omit it. This included an unused volatile mail read inside `__DSP_boot_task` and a force-active debug string for `__DSP_add_task`.

key_files.reason: Changed the Dolphin `dsp` library object list so `dolphin/dsp/dsp.c` and `dolphin/dsp/dsp_task.c` are built as `Object(Matching, ...)` instead of `Object(NonMatching, ...)`.
Adjusted conditional compilation around match-sensitive code in `__DSP_boot_task` and the force-active `__DSP_add_task` debug string from `MUST_MATCH` to `!BUGFIX`.

what_changed: In `src/dolphin/dsp/dsp_task.c`, an unused volatile read from `DSPReadMailFromDSP()` in `__DSP_boot_task` is now compiled under `#ifndef BUGFIX` instead of `#ifdef MUST_MATCH`.

In `src/dolphin/dsp/dsp_task.c`, the `#pragma force_active on` static debug string `"__DSP_add_task() : Added task    : 0x%08X\n"` is now compiled under `#ifndef BUGFIX` instead of `#ifdef MUST_MATCH`.
smart_moves: The PR did not only flip build status to `Matching`; it also moved match-required artifacts from a `MUST_MATCH`-only path to the normal non-bugfix path, making the matching source self-sufficient for relinking.

Using `#ifndef BUGFIX` documents that the unused hardware/mailbox read and force-active string are required for matching the original binary but may be excluded when intentionally building a bugfixed variant.
The source adjustment is minimal: two preprocessor guard changes support the relink without broad code churn.
decomp_lessons: Before marking objects as `Matching` in `configure.py`, verify that all match-only side effects, unused reads, and force-active data are present in the default matching configuration.

For decomp code that contains original-binary artifacts but questionable runtime behavior, prefer guarding them with `#ifndef BUGFIX` when they should remain in matching builds and disappear only from bugfix builds.
Unused assignments can be required for matching, especially when volatile hardware reads such as `DSPReadMailFromDSP()` may affect generated code or preserve original behavior.
Force-active strings can be part of the binary layout and may need to remain compiled even if they appear unused.
naming_conventions: Object matching status is expressed as `Object(Matching, "path/to/file.c")` or `Object(NonMatching, "path/to/file.c")`.
Bugfix-variant exclusions are represented with `#ifndef BUGFIX` rather than `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` when code should be present in normal matching builds.
Internal Dolphin DSP functions in this slice use double-underscore names such as `__DSP_boot_task` and `__DSP_add_task`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promote objects from `NonMatching` to `Matching` only after source-level conditional compilation matches the expected relink behavior.
Preserve unused volatile reads with a scoped local such as `vu32 msg = DSPReadMailFromDSP();` when they are needed to reproduce original code generation.
Use `#pragma push`, `#pragma force_active on`, and static string data to force retention of otherwise unused debug strings that affect binary matching.
Replace `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` with `#ifndef BUGFIX` when a construct should be included by default for matching and excluded only for bugfix builds.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that replaced `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` with `#ifndef BUGFIX` to preserve matching artifacts in default builds.
Find Dolphin library PRs that relinked objects by changing `Object(NonMatching, ...)` to `Object(Matching, ...)` in `configure.py`.
Search for other force-active debug strings in Dolphin code that are required for matching.
Search for unused volatile hardware reads guarded by `BUGFIX` or `MUST_MATCH`.
